subject: [PATCH] btrfs: release extent lock per folio in readahead

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary: Reviewer Qu Wenruo noted that the patch does not address a potential issue where compressed readahead can expand the RA range, resulting in folios without extent locks, but assured that direct reads on compressed extents will always fallback to buffered reads and thus there is no window to race with direct reads.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good to me.

Reviewed-by: Qu Wenruo <wqu@suse.com>


Just to mention that Sashiko gave a review with level high, which 
doesn't seem to be caused by this patch itself, and seems to be a false 
alert.

Btrfs compressed readahead can expand the RA range, resulting folios 
that has no extent lock at all.
Thus in theory we still can have some folios without extent lock for 
compressed readahead.

But thankfully direct read on compressed extent will always fallback to 
buffered read, thus there is no window to race with direct reads.

Thanks,
Qu

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary: Reviewer Qu Wenruo noted that unlocking the extent lock for a folio in readahead can still cause issues if other threads are holding locks on overlapping ranges, and suggested adding an extra check to ensure the unlock is only done within the original readahead range.
reviews.raw_body:
Wait, this is still problematic, that we can clear the EXTENT_LOCK for 
ranges that is held by other threads.

The read itself is protected by folio lock so we won't race, but some 
other readers may got their extent lock unlocked still seems problematic.

Can we do an extra check on the folio range and only unlock if it's 
inside the original RA range?

Thanks,
Qu

2026-06-13.patch_summary: This patch modifies the btrfs readahead function to release extent locks per folio, rather than holding them for the entire readahead loop. This change is intended to prevent a race condition between readahead and direct IO writes that was introduced in kernel version 6.14. The patch reverts the behavior of locking/unlocking extents as a unit around the readahead loop, which was changed in kernel version 6.12. By releasing extent locks per folio, overlapping reads can proceed without blocking on the extent lock.
